title: Super Admin recovery UX and reliable role resolution in Admin Dashboard

requirements.summary: Show logged-in Principal ID on Admin Dashboard with copy-to-clipboard control that renders even when role is Unknown/errors.
acceptanceCriteria: When logged in, the Admin Dashboard shows a "Principal ID" line containing the full principal text.
A user can copy the Principal ID via a visible UI control (e.g., button) and receives a clear success indication (e.g., "Copied").
This Principal ID display renders regardless of whether the backend role query returns "Unknown"/errors.

file_operations.description: Add a persistent "Principal ID" display sourced from the Internet Identity identity object (identity.getPrincipal().toString()) plus a copy-to-clipboard Button. Include clear English feedback state (e.g., "Copied" / copy failed) and ensure this section renders even if role/permissions queries are loading, errored, or return "Unknown".

requirements.summary: Add a Super Admin Recovery section to Admin Dashboard for non-SuperAdmin logged-in users to trigger backend recovery and show clear success/error outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: If the logged-in user is not Super Admin, the Admin Dashboard shows a "Super Admin Recovery" section.
Clicking a recovery button runs the recovery/initialization action and, on success, the dashboard updates to show Role: SuperAdmin and unlocks Super Admin-only cards.
If the canister is already initialized to a different Super Admin, the UI shows a clear English error message explaining that only the current Super Admin can change Super Admin access.

file_operations.description: Add a React Query mutation hook (e.g., useRecoverSuperAdmin) that calls the backend capability recoverSuperAdmin(), returns the backend message, and exposes loading/error state for UI. Ensure it can be used to trigger role/permission query invalidations on success.

Add a "Super Admin Recovery" section that is only visible when identity is present and the current role is not "SuperAdmin". Provide a recovery Button that triggers the new useRecoverSuperAdmin mutation and renders clear English success/error messaging. On success, invalidate/refetch role and permissions queries so the dashboard updates to Role: SuperAdmin and Super Admin-only cards render.

requirements.summary: Ensure a shared (non-query) role-initializing call runs after successful Internet Identity login so the Admin Dashboard consistently resolves the correct role without manual refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: After logging in via the Header "Admin Login" flow, the Admin Dashboard consistently shows the correct role (e.g., SuperAdmin/AdminUser/User) without requiring manual refreshes.
The UI no longer commonly displays transient "Unknown" role due to missing initialization calls when the canister is not yet initialized.

file_operations.description: Add a dedicated React Query mutation hook (e.g., useInitializeAdminSession) that performs a shared backend call intended to initialize/refresh authorization state after login (for example by invoking an existing shared backend capability used for role initialization) and returns status for the UI to act upon.

Trigger the shared post-login initialization mutation when the page is reached with an authenticated identity (run once per identity/session), then invalidate/refetch ['callerRole'] and ['callerPermissions'] so Role reliably resolves to the correct value instead of remaining "Unknown"/"Guest". Keep all user-facing text introduced by this change in English.
